meta.context_preservation: This file preserves the complete context for iterative development

project_context.objective: Integration of the BYON system with infinite memory (FHRSS+FCPE) as orchestrator on top of the OpenClaw platform
project_context.key_principle: OpenClaw = Platform, BYON = Orchestrator - both coexist

capabilities.fault_tolerance: 100% recovery at 50% data loss via FHRSS

byon_reference.note: Built incorrectly (OpenClaw over BYON) - we do the inverse

current_state._v0_6_4_note: Historical snapshot. Current authoritative status is docs/RESEARCH_PROGRESS_v0.6.md (v0.6.0 -> v0.6.4, Level 2 of 4).
current_state.phase: HISTORICAL - v0.1/v0.2 phases completed 2026-02-02; v0.6.x research line ongoing
current_state.next_action: Continue v0.6.x research line - see RESEARCH_PROGRESS_v0.6.md

1.1.name: Create BYON directory structure
1.1.notes: Includes GMV integration (task 5.7) with GlobalMemoryHint and Auditor GMV gate

1.2.name: Configure BYON-OpenClaw integration points
1.2.notes: OpenClawBridge (SINGLE platform constraint), ChannelAdapter, MemoryBridge (FHRSS+FCPE primary)

1.3.name: Create unified configuration
1.3.notes: Full ByonSystemConfig with system, openclaw, byon, security, audit, executor sections

2.1.name: Create Python memory service
2.1.notes: FastAPI server wrapping FHRSS+FCPE with typed memory handlers

2.2.name: Enhance TypeScript memory client
2.2.notes: Full MemoryClient with retry logic, timeout handling, all AgentMemory API methods

2.3.name: Implement memory context manager
2.3.notes: ContextManager for EvidencePack, FactExtractor with regex patterns, SimilaritySearch semantic/keyword/hybrid

2.4.name: Create memory initialization and health checks
2.4.notes: HARD STOP (exit 10) if memory unavailable. MemoryHealth with alert callbacks.

3.1.name: Implement EvidencePack builder
3.1.notes: Enhanced EvidencePackBuilder with auto-extraction, memory integration, codebase context helpers, forbidden data detection

3.2.name: Implement PlanDraft generator
3.2.notes: PlanDraftBuilder with auto risk level, ActionGenerator with intent parsing, RiskAssessor with factor-based scoring

3.3.name: Implement ApprovalRequest creator
3.3.notes: ApprovalRequestBuilder with plan integration, SecurityChecker with forbidden patterns, dangerous commands, path traversal, injection detection

3.4.name: Implement ExecutionOrder with Ed25519 signing
3.4.notes: Ed25519Signer with key generation/verification, KeyManager with encrypted storage, ExecutionOrderBuilder requiring signed orders

3.5.name: Implement JohnsonReceipt generator
3.5.notes: JohnsonReceiptBuilder with action result tracking, error handling, verification status, execution summary generation

4.1.name: Implement action whitelist
4.1.notes: ActionWhitelist with configurable policies, ForbiddenPathsChecker with 6 categories, ForbiddenPatternsChecker with network/process/eval/traversal/filesystem patterns

4.2.name: Implement risk assessment
4.2.notes: RiskAssessmentSystem with weighted factor scoring, thresholds: low ≤30, medium ≤60, high >60

4.3.name: Implement resource limits by risk
4.3.notes: ResourceLimitsManager with real-time tracking, limit checking, usage percentage calculation

4.4.name: Implement BYON Style Contract validator
4.4.notes: BYONStyleValidator with 7 forbidden categories, ValidateOrRegenerateController with auto-fix, JSON Schema for style contract, CompositePolicyChecker

5.1.name: Implement immutable audit trail
5.1.notes: HashChain with SHA-256, DocumentStateManager with lifecycle states, AuditService with auto-checkpoints

5.2.name: Implement calendar indexing
5.2.notes: Hierarchical year/month/week/day/hour indexing with time range queries

5.3.name: Implement daily digest generator
5.3.notes: DailyDigestGenerator with summary metrics, IntegratedAuditSystem combining all audit components

5.5.name: Add JSON Schemas for GMV outputs

5.6.name: Add TS validators for GMV schemas

6.1.name: Implement Worker Agent
6.1.notes: WorkerAgent with InboxWatcher, PlanGenerator, MemoryHandler, GMV integration

6.2.name: Implement Auditor Agent
6.2.notes: AuditorAgent with Ed25519 signing, ApprovalManager with auto-approve for low risk

6.3.name: Implement Executor Agent
6.3.notes: ExecutorAgent AIR-GAPPED - shell_exec disabled, path traversal prevention, signature verification required

6.4.name: Implement handoff system
6.4.notes: HandoffManager with file-based channels, FileWatcher, Serializer, OrchestratorHandoffController

7.1.name: Create OpenClaw-BYON bridge
7.1.notes: MessageAdapter orchestrates full flow: OpenClaw -> Worker -> Auditor -> Executor -> Response. Auto-approve low risk, approval requests, channel-specific formatting.

7.2.name: Integrate with OpenClaw memory system
7.2.notes: ByonMemoryProvider wraps FHRSS+FCPE as primary with OpenClaw MemoryIndexManager fallback. Unified search interface, evidence building support, recovery testing capabilities.

7.3.name: Create approval UI in OpenClaw
7.3.notes: Lit Element components following OpenClaw patterns. Controller handles state/gateway. View renders approval cards, detail overlay, risk badges, security checks, expiration countdown.

7.4.name: Create CLI commands for BYON
7.4.notes: Full CLI with approve, watch, status, history, inbox commands. Supports --json output and color-coded terminal display.

8.1.name: Create Docker configuration
8.1.notes: Multi-stage Dockerfile with --target for worker/auditor/executor. Memory-service uses FastAPI with mounted FHRSS+FCPE. Executor runs air-gapped with network_mode:none. Redis and watcher included.

8.2.name: Create startup scripts
8.2.notes: start-byon.sh with pre-flight checks, directory setup, key generation, health wait. stop-byon.sh with graceful/hard stop. health-check.sh with JSON output, watch mode. setup-keys.sh with Ed25519 generation.

9.1.name: Create unit tests
9.1.notes: Unit tests for protocol (EvidencePack, PlanDraft, ExecutionOrder, JohnsonReceipt, HashChain), policy (ActionWhitelist, ForbiddenPaths, ForbiddenPatterns, RiskAssessment, ResourceLimits), memory (MemoryClient, FactExtractor, ContextManager, MemoryHealth), crypto (Ed25519Signer, HashComputation, KeyManager, SignatureVerificationChain). Vitest with 70% coverage thresholds.

9.2.name: Create integration tests
9.2.notes: Integration tests for Worker->Auditor, Executor flow, Memory system (FHRSS+FCPE), Handoff system, Full OpenClaw->BYON flow

9.3.name: Create security tests
9.3.notes: Security tests for Ed25519 signatures, path traversal prevention, policy enforcement, hash chain integrity

9.4.name: Create documentation
9.4.notes: Complete documentation: Architecture, Quick Start, Security model, API reference

openclaw_agents.note: OpenClaw agents remain unchanged - BYON sits on top

decisions_made.decision: BYON as orchestrator over OpenClaw platform
decisions_made.rationale: Preserves all OpenClaw functionality while adding BYON intelligence layer

decisions_made.decision: FHRSS+FCPE as primary, memory-core as fallback

decisions_made.rationale: Decoupled agents, easy debugging, Docker compatible
Maximum security - executor cannot access network

decisions_made.decision: Runtime policy gates (platform-gate.ts, execution-gate.ts) + validator checks
decisions_made.rationale: Prevents architectural drift - platform constraints enforced at build time (validator) and runtime (gates)

decisions_made.decision: GMV as non-decision metadata daemon in Layer 5
decisions_made.rationale: Maintains emergent global memory state without autonomy. Read-only on FHRSS+FCPE, write-only metadata. Zero executor/channel/network access.

notes_and_learnings.content: System tested with 100% recovery at 50% data loss. Compression ratio 100x documented. All scientific tests passed.
Extension memory-fhrss-fcpe exists but only provides basic store/search. Needs full AgentMemory API.
D:\Github Repo\Byon_bot has complete MACP implementation but was built incorrectly (OpenClaw over BYON). Use as reference only.

how_to_resume: 1. Read this BYON_PROGRESSION.json file to understand current state
3. Read BYON_EXECUTION_PLAN.json for detailed task specifications
4. Continue from the next pending task in the current phase
5. Update this file after each completed task
context_files: BYON_EXECUTION_PLAN.json - Full technical plan with all tasks and specifications
BYON_PROGRESSION.json - This file - tracks progress and preserves context
key_reminders: OpenClaw must remain untouched - BYON integrates through bridge
BYON orchestrator MUST NOT START fără memory service
All execution orders must be signed with Ed25519

actions: Analyzed Global Memory Vitalizer (GMV) specification from ChatGPT

decisions_made: GMV approved as Layer 5 component (memory state daemon)
GMV constraints: zero executor/channel/network access, read-only memory, write-only metadata
GMV provides structural bias to Worker/Auditor, not decisions
Attractor = deterministic cluster from embeddings (SHA256 ID)
GlobalMemorySummary = structural state, not textual summary
Coherence formula: 1 - (attractors / support)
Entropy levels: stable (>0.75), rising (0.4-0.75), fragmented (<0.4)
next_steps: Begin PHASE_1 - Create BYON directory structure
Integrate GMV read-only API in EvidencePack (task 5.7)

ExecutionOrder MUST be signed before build() completes

decisions_made: ActionWhitelist: file_delete and shell_exec disabled by default, require explicit enable
ForbiddenPathsChecker: 6 categories (system, credential, vcs, package, lock_file, build)
ForbiddenPatternsChecker: 5 pattern types (network, process, eval, traversal, filesystem) with risk levels
RiskAssessmentSystem: weighted factors (fileDeletes: 0.20, sensitivity: 0.15, rollback: 0.15)
ResourceLimitsManager: tracks iterations, time, memory, disk, file operations per risk level
BYONStyleValidator: 7 forbidden categories (psychology, empathy, stories, meta, filler, hedging, apologetic)
ValidateOrRegenerateController: supports auto-fix rules and regeneration callback
CompositePolicyChecker: combines whitelist, path, pattern, risk, and resource limit checks

decisions_made: HashChain: SHA-256 algorithm with genesis hash, tamper-evident verification
DocumentStateManager: 6 states (draft, pending, approved, executed, failed, cancelled) with transition rules
AuditService: Combines hash chain and state manager with auto-checkpoints and verification
CalendarIndex: Hierarchical year/month/week/day/hour indexing for fast time-based queries
DailyDigestGenerator: Summary metrics, hourly activity, document digests, state changes, errors, highlights
IntegratedAuditSystem: Composite class combining all audit components

actions: Completed PHASE_6 - BYON Orchestrator Core (all 4 tasks)
Task 6.1: Created Worker Agent with inbox-watcher.ts, plan-generator.ts, memory-handler.ts, index.ts
Task 6.2: Created Auditor Agent with signer.ts (Ed25519), approval-manager.ts, index.ts
Task 6.3: Created Executor Agent (AIR-GAPPED) with signature-verifier.ts, action-handlers.ts, receipt-generator.ts, index.ts
Task 6.4: Created handoff system with serializer.ts, file-watcher.ts, manager.ts, index.ts
All three MACP agents now fully implemented: Worker, Auditor, Executor
File-based handoff system enables decoupled agent communication

Updated BYON_PROGRESSION.json - PHASE_6 marked as COMPLETED (100%)
decisions_made: Worker: InboxWatcher polls directory, MemoryHandler manages FHRSS+FCPE context, PlanGenerator creates drafts
Auditor: ExecutionOrderSigner uses Ed25519 with key pair, ApprovalManager auto-approves low risk
Executor: AIR-GAPPED - shell_exec disabled, path traversal blocked, signature verification mandatory
Handoff: File-based with serialize/deserialize, 4 channels (worker->auditor, auditor->user, auditor->executor, executor->worker)
ReceiptGenerator creates JohnsonReceipt for ALL execution results including rejections
